children.heading: .—Submission of Applications
children.label: a .—Submission of Applications
children.text: If a sponsor intends to sell or lease a general aviation airport or lease any other type of airport for a long term to a person (other than a public agency), the sponsor and purchaser or lessee may apply to the Secretary of Transportation for exemptions under this section.

children.label: i
children.text: in the case of a primary airport, by at least 65 percent of the scheduled air carriers serving the airport and by scheduled and nonscheduled air carriers whose aircraft landing at the airport during the preceding calendar year, had a total landed weight during the preceding calendar year of at least 65 percent of the total landed weight of all aircraft landing at the airport during such year; or

children.label: ii
children.text: in the case of a nonprimary airport, by the Secretary after the airport has consulted with at least 65 percent of the owners of aircraft based at that airport, as determined by the Secretary.

children.heading: .—Objection to exemption
children.label: B .—Objection to exemption
children.text: An air carrier shall be deemed to have approved a sponsor’s application for an exemption under subparagraph (A) unless the air carrier has submitted an objection, in writing, to the sponsor within 60 days of the filing of the sponsor’s application with the Secretary, or within 60 days of the service of the application upon that air carrier, whichever is later.

children.heading: .—Landed weight defined
children.label: C .—Landed weight defined
children.text: In this paragraph, the term “landed weight” means the weight of aircraft transporting passengers or cargo, or both, in intrastate, interstate, and foreign air transportation, as the Secretary determines under regulations the Secretary prescribes.

children.heading: .—Repayment requirements
children.label: 2 .—Repayment requirements
children.text: If the Secretary grants an exemption to a sponsor pursuant to paragraph (1), the Secretary shall grant an exemption to the sponsor from the provisions of sections 47107 and 47152 of this title (and any other law, regulation, or grant assurance) to the extent necessary to waive any obligation of the sponsor to repay to the Federal Government any grants, or to return to the Federal Government any property, received by the airport under this title, the Airport and Airway Improvement Act of 1982, or any other law.

children.heading: .—Compensation from airport operations
children.label: 3 .—Compensation from airport operations
children.text: If the Secretary grants an exemption to a sponsor pursuant to paragraph (1), the Secretary shall grant an exemption to the corresponding purchaser or lessee from the provisions of sections 47107(b) and 47133 of this title (and any other law, regulation, or grant assurance) to the extent necessary to permit the purchaser or lessee to earn compensation from the operations of the airport.

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: A .—In general
children.text: Prior to approving an application submitted under subsection (a), the Secretary may require a benefit-cost analysis.

children.label: i
children.text: be issued not later than 60 days after the date on which the sponsor submits all information required by the Secretary;

children.label: ii
children.text: be based upon a collaborative review process that includes the sponsor or a representative of the sponsor;

children.label: iii
children.text: not constitute the issuance of a Federal grant or obligation to issue a grant under this chapter or other provision of law; and

children.label: iv
children.text: not constitute any other obligation on the part of the Federal Government until the conditions specified in the final benefit-cost analysis are met.
The airport will continue to be available for public use on reasonable terms and conditions and without unjust discrimination.

The operation of the airport will not be interrupted in the event that the purchaser or lessee becomes insolvent or seeks or becomes subject to any State or Federal bankruptcy, reorganization, insolvency, liquidation, or dissolution proceeding or any petition or similar law seeking the dissolution or reorganization of the purchaser or lessee or the appointment of a receiver, trustee, custodian, or liquidator for the purchaser or lessee or a substantial part of the purchaser or lessee’s property, assets, or business.

The purchaser or lessee will maintain, improve, and modernize the facilities of the airport through capital investments and will submit to the Secretary a plan for carrying out such maintenance, improvements, and modernization.

children.label: A
children.text: by at least 65 percent of the air carriers serving the airport; and

children.label: B
children.text: by air carriers whose aircraft landing at the airport during the preceding calendar year had a total landed weight during the preceding calendar year of at least 65 percent of the total landed weight of all aircraft landing at the airport during such year.
The percentage increase in fees imposed on general aviation aircraft at the airport will not exceed the percentage increase in fees imposed on air carriers at the airport.
Safety and security at the airport will be maintained at the highest possible levels.
The adverse effects of noise from operations at the airport will be mitigated to the same extent as at a public airport.
Any adverse effects on the environment from airport operations will be mitigated to the same extent as at a public airport.
Any collective bargaining agreement that covers employees of the airport and is in effect on the date of the sale or lease of the airport will not be abrogated by the sale or lease.

children.heading: .—Multiple airports
children.label: 1 .—Multiple airports
children.text: The Secretary may consider applications under this section submitted by a public airport sponsor for multiple airports under the control of the sponsor if all airports under the control of the sponsor are located in the same State.

children.heading: .—Partial privatization
children.label: 2 .—Partial privatization
children.text: A purchaser or lessee may be an entity in which a sponsor has an interest.

children.heading: .—Required Finding That Approval Will Not Result in Unfair Methods of Competition
children.label: e .—Required Finding That Approval Will Not Result in Unfair Methods of Competition
children.text: The Secretary may approve an application under subsection (b) only if the Secretary finds that the approval will not result in unfair and deceptive practices or unfair methods of competition.

children.heading: .—Interests of General Aviation Users
children.label: f .—Interests of General Aviation Users
children.text: In approving an application of an airport under this section, the Secretary shall ensure that the interests of general aviation users of the airport are not adversely affected.

children.heading: .—Passenger Facility Fees; Apportionments; Service Charges
children.label: g .—Passenger Facility Fees; Apportionments; Service Charges

children.text: imposing a passenger facility charge under ;section 40117 of this title
receiving apportionments under ; orsection 47114 of this title
collecting reasonable rental charges, landing fees, and other service charges from aircraft operators under .section 40116(e)(2) of this title

children.heading: .—Effectiveness of Exemptions
children.label: h .—Effectiveness of Exemptions
children.text: An exemption granted under subsection (b) shall continue in effect only so long as the facilities sold or leased continue to be used for airport purposes.

children.heading: .—Revocation of Exemptions
children.label: i .—Revocation of Exemptions
children.text: The Secretary may revoke an exemption issued to a purchaser or lessee of an airport under subsection (b)(3) if, after providing the purchaser or lessee with notice and an opportunity to be heard, the Secretary determines that the purchaser or lessee has knowingly violated any of the terms specified in subsection (c) for the sale or lease of the airport.

children.heading: .—Nonapplication of Provisions to Airports Owned by Public Agencies
children.label: j .—Nonapplication of Provisions to Airports Owned by Public Agencies
children.text: The provisions of this section requiring the approval of air carriers in determinations concerning the use of revenues, and imposition of fees, at an airport shall not be extended so as to apply to any airport owned by a public agency that is not participating in the program established by this section.

children.heading: .—Audits
children.label: k .—Audits
children.text: The Secretary may conduct periodic audits of the financial records and operations of an airport receiving an exemption under this section.

children.heading: .—Predevelopment Limitation
children.label: l .—Predevelopment Limitation
children.text: A grant to an airport sponsor under this subchapter for predevelopment planning costs relating to the preparation of an application or proposed application under this section may not exceed $750,000 per application or proposed application.
